No pulmonary embolism and no acute intrathoracic process. 2. Severe emphysema WS LOCATION: DHS16994 Narrative CTA CHEST FOR PULMONARY EMBOLISM W CONTRAST HISTORY: hypoxia COMPARISON: None. Automated exposure control was also utilized to decrease patient radiation dose. TECHNIQUE: Helical tomographic images of the chest were obtained after the administration of intravenous contrast following angiogram protocol. Additionally, 3D and multiplanar reformatted images were provided. FINDINGS: Heart size is normal. There are some coronary calcifications. No pulmonary embolism. No intrathoracic lymphadenopathy. No pericardial effusion. No consolidation within the lungs. No pleural fluid and no pneumothorax. No suspicious lung masses or lung nodules. Severe emphysema. Within the visualized upper abdomen, no acute findings. Moderate hiatal hernia. No acute osseous findings.
